highlights.text: One of those cycles is that after “elevator down” selloffs like we saw last Friday, ES usually follows with a vicious short squeeze or relief rally to re-test some of the levels that failed on the way down.

After last Friday’s non-stop sell, I warned readers about a squeeze to start this week, writing on Friday at 4pm: “As readers know, elevator down days like today have a closely related sibling: The Short Squeeze and its inevitable will we retrace a good chunk of this sell.

I wrote yesterday at 4pm when we were 5740: “We may not be done with 5886 yet - it is common for these magnet levels to test many times and even though we tested it Friday, yesterday, and put in two failed breakdowns today, do not be shocked if it makes a few more appearances this week”.
As readers know, by “this general area” I was referring to the 5886 level, which would be this weeks critical magnet to trade.
Every month typically has a magnet level that price operates around, and traps below over and over.
Unsurprisingly, sold back to the 5886 magnet by 10am this morning.

post_title: Will Nvidia Earnings Make Or Break The SPX Rally? Nov 21 Plan
